title: Write a Service Control Policy
description:
## Guardrail: Deny Disabling CloudTrail

SCPs are JSON guardrails that cap what member accounts can do — even their admins.

**Your task:** write an SCP at **`/root/scp.json`** that **denies** the actions **`cloudtrail:StopLogging`** and **`cloudtrail:DeleteTrail`** on all resources, so no one in a member account can disable audit logging.

> 💡 An SCP is an IAM-style policy document: `Version`, then a `Statement` with `Effect: "Deny"`, the `Action` list, and `Resource: "*"`.

hints.title: Deny statement
hints.body: Use Effect Deny with the two CloudTrail actions and a wildcard resource.

validation.description: Checks for a valid policy with a Deny statement covering the CloudTrail actions.

commands.description: Valid JSON with a Deny statement for cloudtrail:StopLogging
commands.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/root/scp.json')); s=d['Statement']; s=s if isinstance(s,list) else [s]; ok=any(st.get('Effect')=='Deny' and 'cloudtrail:StopLogging' in (st['Action'] if isinstance(st['Action'],list) else [st['Action']]) for st in s); print('ok' if ok else 'no')"
